text.title: perf-test-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for perf-test-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Caching every function with @lru_cache after profiling shows "DB calls are slow."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: lru_cache works for pure functions; it breaks for stateful operations (DB calls, time-dependent results, user-specific data). The reflex to add caching without checking purity is the #1 agent gotcha.

failure-mode.description: Premature optimization: agents will rewrite a function for "perf" without a benchmark.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Refuse to merge without before/after numbers from the same machine.

failure-mode.description: "Add caching" reflex: agents wrap everything in @lru_cache — works for pure functions, breaks for stateful ones (DB calls, time-dependent results).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Check purity before caching; profile to verify the function is actually a bottleneck.

failure-mode.description: Locust spawn-rate: agents set spawn_rate = users and overwhelm the target before the test even starts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a ramp profile (e.g. 10/s) to warm up gradually.

failure-mode.description: Agents read pytest-benchmark mean and ignore stddev; force fail criteria on mean ± 2σ of baseline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: High-stddev results are unreliable; always consider statistical variance.

failure-mode.description: N+1 detection: agents use the caplog snippet and miss the actual queries because logging is set to INFO.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use the SQLAlchemy event listener fixture instead of log-based detection.

failure-mode.description: Profiling: agents profile localhost with no network latency and conclude the service is fast.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a realistic latency simulation (tc qdisc add dev lo root netem delay 20ms).

failure-mode.description: Memory: memory_profiler @profile works only on the decorated function; agents miss leaks elsewhere.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use memray run for the whole process instead.

failure-mode.description: Agents will load-test prod by accident.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard-code an env-name allowlist (assert "prod" not in TARGET_URL).

failure-mode.description: Threshold drift: agents push the threshold up after every regression to keep CI green.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a human approver for threshold changes.
